title: Garlicky Algerian Garlic Tagine
description: A rich, dense, and garlicky stew originating from Algeria, featuring a blend of sauteed garlic, aromatic spices, and tender meat. This dish is perfect for those who love intense flavors and hearty portions.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the cumin, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and thyme. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth, bring to a boil, then cover the tagine or Dutch oven with a lid.
Transfer the tagine or Dutch oven to the oven and cook at 300°F (150°C) for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the tagine or Dutch oven from the oven and stir in the chopped parsley.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
